examples.title: Basic Pointer and Reference Declaration
examples.description: Demonstrates how to declare and use pointers and reference variables in C++
examples.explanation:
1. int* ptr declares a pointer to an integer
2. &num gets the memory address of num
3. int& ref creates a reference (alias) to num
4. *ptr dereferences the pointer to access the value
5. References are aliases - modifying ref changes num directly

examples.title: Pointers and Arrays with Function Parameters
examples.description: Shows how pointers work with arrays and passing by reference to functions
examples.explanation:
1. doubleValue takes a pointer parameter - must pass address with &
2. tripleValue takes a reference parameter - no & needed in call
3. Arrays decay to pointers when passed to functions
4. Pointer arithmetic: *(ptr + i) accesses element at index i
5. Both pointers and references enable call-by-reference in functions

examples.title: Dynamic Memory Allocation and Smart Pointers
examples.description: Advanced example covering heap memory, pointers to pointers, and smart pointers
examples.explanation:
1. new allocates heap memory, delete frees it
2. new[] allocates array on heap, delete[] frees it
3. Pointer to pointer (int**) stores address of another pointer
4. unique_ptr: exclusive ownership, automatically deleted when out of scope
5. shared_ptr: reference counting, deleted when count reaches 0
6. weak_ptr: non-owning reference, can be expired or locked to access
